steps.arg: If you want to see more examples , see  List Of RSCG
My name is Andrei Ignat and I am deeply fond of Roslyn Source Code Generator.

Today I will present Najlot.Audit.SourceGenerator . Generates audit/change-tracking code at compile time — detects which properties changed between two snapshots of an object. How to use   1. Mark your entity and a partial provider with [AuditProvider]:```charp   [AuditProvider]   public partial class Person   {       public string FirstName { get; set; } = string.Empty;       public string LastName { get; set; } = string.Empty;       public int Id { get; set; }   }      [AuditProvider]   public partial class PersonAuditProvider   {       [AuditIgnore(nameof(Person.Id))]              public static partial IEnumerable<PropertyValue> GetPropertyValues(Person entity);   }```   2. Take a snapshot, mutate, then get changes:```charp   var audit = new Audit();   audit.RegisterProvider<PersonAuditProvider>();   var snapshot = audit.CreateSnapshot(p);      p.LastName = "Ignat";      foreach (var change in snapshot.GetChanges())       Console.WriteLine($"{change.Path}: {change.OldValue} → {change.NewValue}");```  You can use for  Property-level change tracking / audit logs at compile time.   Supports [AuditIgnore] to exclude properties. .
You can download the code from here
So , let's start the project with Visual Studio Code
To use it ,you will put the Nuget Najlot.Audit.SourceGenerator into the csproj
And now I will show you an example of using Najlot.Audit.SourceGenerator
